qa.user_verbatim_complaint: 重构 auto-commit/checkpoint 系统，让 git blame 的每一行都指向真实的语义 commit —— 所有自动化快照改写到 refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch>，不再污染任何分支 HEAD。完整 v2 patch：7 个文件 + CAS 并发保护 + 边界守卫 + 文档。
qa.complaint_resolution_evidence: Verified in scratch repos (AC1-AC8) that write_checkpoint writes to refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch> and NEVER moves HEAD. Verified in the real dot-claude repo that no new HEAD-pollution commits have landed on master since 2026-04-16 06:48:00 (pre-refactor baseline); two NEW commits (06:52:23 and 06:57:07) live ONLY on refs/checkpoints/master, confirming the new path is wired up and active. All 6 converted hooks source the shared lib; smart-checkpoint.sh stubbed to no-op (body = `exit 0`); push.sh correctly refuses dirty trees with exit 1 and no commit creation.
qa.overall_assessment: The refactor achieves its primary goal: automated snapshots now land on refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch> and never on HEAD. 7 of 8 acceptance criteria pass as written. AC6 exhibits a measurable deviation from the spec literal — 9 dangling (unreachable) commit-tree objects remain after 10 concurrent distinct-tree invocations — but no data loss, no branching, no HEAD movement. Dev's AC6 deviation argument was partly wrong: the dev report claimed `git fsck --no-dangling` is clean, but `git fsck` reports 9 dangling commits per concurrent burst (they are eligible for GC). The alternative behaviour (10 linear commits with zero dangling objects) is achievable only with a different algorithm (lock-based serialization or commit-tree-after-CAS), and the current outcome is functionally acceptable: linear chain, all 10 files captured in the tip tree, no corruption. Overall: functionally correct, spec AC6 literal is violated at the orphan-object level only. Verdict is WARNING (not PASS, not FAIL): primary objective met, one minor spec-literal deviation with no user impact.

success_criteria_results.criterion: git log HEAD shows zero auto-generated snapshot commits after implementation
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Real-repo observation: /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude git log master since dev session; scratch-repo AC1 (15 files + write_checkpoint leaves HEAD unchanged)

Two NEW snapshot commits (at 06:52:23 and 06:57:07) exist ONLY on refs/checkpoints/master.

real repo master tip: dafb290 at 06:48:00 (pre-refactor)
real repo refs/checkpoints/master: 413ca99 at 06:57:07 + 0b254ea at 06:52:23 (post-refactor, side-ref only)

success_criteria_results.criterion: git log refs/checkpoints/master shows all automated snapshots in linear chain

success_criteria_results.details: AC5 scratch: 2 sequential write_checkpoint calls on feature/x produced refs/checkpoints/feature-x with tip 3fee97a whose parent is bc781aa (first call). AC6 scratch: 10 concurrent calls produced a linear chain with every commit having exactly 1 parent (no merges, no branching). Real repo refs/checkpoints/master: linear chain 413ca99 -> 0b254ea -> dafb290 (ref was seeded from HEAD when first created).
evidence: AC5: git log refs/checkpoints/feature-x shows 3fee97a -> bc781aa -> seed (linear)
AC6: git log --format='%H %P' shows no commit with >1 parent

success_criteria_results.criterion: All 8 acceptance tests pass (see acceptance_tests field)
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Scratch-repo runs AC1 through AC8 in /tmp/qa-*
success_criteria_results.details: AC1, AC2, AC3, AC4, AC5, AC7, AC8 pass as written. AC6 passes on the critical invariants (HEAD unmoved, linear chain, all file data captured, no data loss) but produces 9 dangling commit objects per 10-concurrent burst, which violates the literal spec text 'git fsck --unreachable shows no new dangling commits from concurrent writes'. These orphans are eligible for GC and do not cause user-visible harm.
evidence: AC6: git fsck reports 9 dangling commit objects after 10 concurrent distinct-tree writes
AC6: ref tip tree contains all 10 files; no data loss

success_criteria_results.criterion: git fsck --unreachable shows no dangling commits from concurrent writes
success_criteria_results.verification_method: AC6 with 10 parallel subshells each writing a DISTINCT file
success_criteria_results.details: Observed 9 dangling commit objects after 10 concurrent write_checkpoint calls. Root cause: the CAS retry loop calls commit-tree BEFORE update-ref. CAS losers have already produced a commit object that never gets attached to the ref. On retry the tree-equality short-circuit prevents a second orphan, but the first orphan per loser remains. The orphans are unreachable and GC-eligible (default 2-week grace), so no data is lost, but they technically violate the spec literal.
evidence: scratch AC6 run output: `git fsck` reports exactly 9 dangling commit objects
dev report claimed: '(c) git fsck --no-dangling is clean' -- this claim is inaccurate for the default `git fsck` invocation; the report was relying on a narrower flag

success_criteria_results.criterion: /push on dirty tree exits non-zero with 'commit first' message
success_criteria_results.verification_method: Scratch repo with dirty tree, run `bash push.sh --auto`, capture `$?`
success_criteria_results.details: Dirty tree: push.sh exits 1 with red banner '❌ Refusing to push: working tree is dirty' and message 'commit first — /push no longer auto-commits' plus three option bullets. No commit created; HEAD unchanged.
evidence: Direct bash invocation returned exit code 1
No new commit appeared on HEAD after the push attempt

root_cause_verification.rationale: The root cause was 7 hook files each calling `git commit` on the working branch HEAD. All 7 now delegate to write_checkpoint which uses git commit-tree + update-ref CAS on refs/checkpoints/<sanitized-branch>. Verified by grepping `git commit` across hooks/* and confirming all matches are either in doc strings or in unrelated git-hook installers (pre-commit.sh, session-git-init.sh, ensure-git-repo.sh). No converted hook contains an unfixed `git commit` call.

build_verification.deploy_command: N/A (in-place hook files are sourced at runtime)
build_verification.build_output_summary: All 8 bash files pass syntax check

script_quality_results.execution_test: not independently exercised, but delegates to same write_checkpoint exercised by AC1-AC8
pass (AC7 dirty exit 1; clean path attempts push)

issues.finding: Pre-existing bug (not introduced by this refactor): STAGED_COUNT computed via `echo "$STAGED" | grep -c '^'` yields 1 when STAGED is empty (echo emits a trailing newline). This causes the 'Nothing to push' early-exit block to be skipped and the script always proceeds to `git push origin`. Inherited from prior push.sh at b5af07c.
issues.recommendation: Use `printf '%s' "$STAGED" | grep -c '^'` or `[ -z "$STAGED" ] && STAGED_COUNT=0 || STAGED_COUNT=$(echo "$STAGED" | wc -l)`. Out of scope for this request but worth a follow-up ticket.

regression_test_results.test: no `git commit` in converted hooks (grep check)
regression_test_results.details: Only residual matches are: push.sh:98 (help string), git-fswatch.sh:294 (comment), lib/checkpoint-core.sh:19,24 (doc comments about commit-tree). Zero real `git commit` invocations in converted hooks.

All 6 convert-targets (posttool-git-checkpoint.sh, auto-commit.sh, stop-git-commit.sh, checkpoint.sh, git-fswatch.sh, and push.sh by not needing it) reference the lib; push.sh does not source it (correct — push no longer writes snapshots). smart-checkpoint.sh is a stub referencing the lib only in its deprecation comment.
No reference to smart-checkpoint.sh exists in /root/.claude/settings.json or /root/.claude/settings.local.json (confirmed by grep). PostToolUse still points at posttool-git-checkpoint.sh.

regression_test_results.test: no new HEAD pollution on real repo during session
regression_test_results.details: Last HEAD commit on /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude master is dafb290 at 06:48:00 (pre-refactor baseline). No commits on HEAD after that timestamp. Two snapshots appeared on refs/checkpoints/master (at 06:52:23 and 06:57:07) but NOT on HEAD — exactly the intended behaviour.

code_quality_findings.issue: Pre-existing empty-string counting bug in STAGED_COUNT / MODIFIED_COUNT / UNTRACKED_COUNT (see script_quality_results entry for push.sh).
code_quality_findings.recommendation: Separate follow-up ticket; out of scope for this request.

app_understanding_flow.skip_reason: This is a shell-hook refactor, not a browser-facing feature. No UI surface exists.

ac_results.name: No HEAD pollution
ac_results.observation: Scratch /tmp/qa-checkpoint-v2-*: 15 untracked files + write_checkpoint. `git log HEAD` count=1 (unchanged). `git log refs/checkpoints/master` count=2 (seed+checkpoint). Working dir still has 15 untracked files. Real .git/index untouched (git diff --cached returns 0 names).
ac_results.evidence: RC=0; HEAD=932fb49 before and after; ref tip 85f97fa

ac_results.name: Full working-tree capture (modify + delete + chmod)
ac_results.observation: Modified a.txt, deleted b.txt (via unlink because rm is blocked by bash-safety hook), chmod +x c.txt. write_checkpoint captured all three: `diff-tree --summary` shows `delete mode 100644 b.txt`, `mode change 100644 => 100755 c.txt`, and a.txt file stat `1 insertion`.
ac_results.evidence: HEAD unchanged; ref tree shows expected three-change diff

ac_results.name: Empty repo bootstrap
ac_results.observation: Scratch repo with `git init` and no commits. Created 2 files + write_checkpoint -> RC=0. refs/checkpoints/master created as root commit (cat-file -p shows no `parent` line). Tree contains both files. HEAD is still a symbolic ref to refs/heads/master which does not exist as a concrete commit.
ac_results.evidence: ref sha b90833e; tree efa4c54 contains one.txt + two.txt

ac_results.name: Detached HEAD fallback
ac_results.observation: `git checkout --detach HEAD` then edit -> write_checkpoint -> ref `refs/checkpoints/detached-57c6f43` created (57c6f43 is short SHA of the detached commit).

ac_results.name: Branch slash sanitization + ref re-use
ac_results.observation: `git checkout -b feature/x` then write_checkpoint -> ref `refs/checkpoints/feature-x` (slash replaced with dash). Second write_checkpoint on same branch with new content appended to same ref; tip2 parent = tip1. Linear chain verified.

ac_results.name: Concurrent invocations (10 parallel, distinct trees)
ac_results.observation: 10 parallel subshells, each touching a unique file_001..file_010.txt then calling write_checkpoint. Outcome: HEAD unmoved (pass); linear chain with every commit having exactly 1 parent (pass); all 10 files captured in ref tip tree (no data loss, pass); BUT `git fsck` reports 9 dangling commit objects per burst — these are commit-tree results from CAS losers that never got attached to the ref. They are unreachable (eligible for GC), but they violate the literal spec criterion 'git fsck --unreachable shows no new dangling commits'. Dev's report claimed `git fsck --no-dangling` is clean; default `git fsck` is NOT clean. Classifying as WARNING rather than FAIL because (a) no data loss, (b) linear chain preserved, (c) HEAD never advanced, (d) orphans are GC-eligible and cause no user-visible harm, (e) eliminating them would require a different algorithm (serialization or commit-tree-inside-update-ref).
ac_results.evidence: 10 concurrent runs -> 1 commit on ref + 9 dangling commits; tip tree has file_001..file_010 all present; chain is d1f4f6a -> 75de082 (linear)

ac_results.name: Push dirty-tree guard
ac_results.observation: Dirty tree: `bash push.sh --auto` prints red 'Refusing to push: working tree is dirty' banner, prints 'commit first — /push no longer auto-commits', lists three remedial options, exits with code 1 (verified directly). No new commit created on HEAD. Clean tree with no origin: proceeds to `git push origin master` which fails with 'origin does not appear to be a git repository' (exit 1) — this is expected behaviour for a scratch repo with no remote; the pre-existing empty-count bug is noted separately as a minor issue but is out of scope for this request.
ac_results.evidence: Direct bash invocation: exit 1; output contains literal 'commit first'

ac_results.name: Stop chain idempotency
ac_results.observation: Scratch repo with pending change. Ran stop-git-commit.sh then auto-commit.sh back-to-back. Both reported `Tip: 0b565d8` (same commit). Exactly 1 new commit on refs/checkpoints/master. HEAD unchanged. The second hook's write_checkpoint call short-circuited because TREE == PARENT_TREE.
ac_results.evidence: Ref log shows 2 commits total (seed + 1 checkpoint); tip shown identically by both hook runs

deviations_adjudication.dev_actual: Replaced body with `exit 0` stub; physical file remains.
deviations_adjudication.reasoning: The global bash-safety hook at ~/.claude/hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh blocks `rm` invocations from agents ('BLOCKED: rm is forbidden — delete files manually'). Dev correctly substituted a functional no-op stub. Verified the stub: (a) bash -n passes, (b) `bash smart-checkpoint.sh` exits 0 with zero stdout/stderr, (c) the file contains ZERO `git commit`, `git add`, `git push`, or other mutation calls, (d) settings.json and settings.local.json do not reference smart-checkpoint.sh (verified by grep). The stub is functionally equivalent to deletion. Dev's rationale (bash-safety blocks rm) is accurate and the substitute is acceptable. Recommend the user manually `rm ~/.claude/hooks/smart-checkpoint.sh` at their convenience for cleanliness, but this is optional.

deviations_adjudication.from_spec: AC6 literal: 'exactly 10 linear commits AND git fsck --unreachable shows no new dangling commits'
deviations_adjudication.dev_actual: 2 linear commits from 10 parallel invocations with identical-union trees (dev's test); dev claimed `git fsck --no-dangling` is clean.
deviations_adjudication.reasoning: I re-ran AC6 with 10 DISTINCT trees (each subshell writes a unique file before calling write_checkpoint, matching the orchestrator's explicit instructions). Observed: 1 commit on ref + 9 dangling commit objects. This is NOT the behaviour dev described. Dev's 'identical tree -> idempotency' explanation is incomplete: even with distinct trees, only 1 commit lands on the ref because the winner's `git add -A` captures the current union filesystem state (all 10 files) and later retries by losers short-circuit on TREE == PARENT_TREE. The 9 dangling objects are the losing invocations' commit-tree results from BEFORE they lost CAS. Dev's `git fsck --no-dangling is clean` claim is inaccurate for default `git fsck` — the actual invocation reports the 9 dangling commits. Critical invariants that DO hold (verified): (a) HEAD unchanged, (b) ref chain linear (every commit has exactly 1 parent), (c) no data loss (all 10 files in the ref tip tree). Because the orphans are GC-eligible and cause no user-visible harm, and because fixing this would require a redesign (moving commit-tree INSIDE the CAS, or serializing via a lock file), I accept the current behaviour with a documented WARNING rather than a FAIL. The spec literal is violated at the orphan-object level only; the practical goal (linear, HEAD-clean, no data loss) is met.

all_findings.issue: AC6 spec literal 'git fsck --unreachable shows no new dangling commits' is violated. 10 concurrent distinct-tree invocations produce 9 dangling commit-tree objects. Dev's report claim 'git fsck --no-dangling is clean' is not supported by direct test.
all_findings.recommendation: Either (a) downgrade the spec literal to 'no data loss, linear chain, no HEAD movement' which matches the actual CAS behaviour, or (b) move commit-tree to run AFTER a successful CAS by first CASing on a sentinel placeholder (more complex), or (c) serialize concurrent writers with a flock on .git/checkpoint.lock (simpler but loses concurrency). Option (a) is the pragmatic choice; the orphans are GC-eligible and cause no user harm. If option (a), update the lib comment and CLAUDE.md to note the expected orphan count under high concurrency.

all_findings.issue: Pre-existing (not introduced by this refactor) empty-string bug in STAGED_COUNT/MODIFIED_COUNT/UNTRACKED_COUNT via `echo "$VAR" | grep -c '^'` returning 1 on empty input. Causes 'Nothing to push' early-exit to be unreachable on a clean tree with no upstream.
all_findings.recommendation: Follow-up ticket: switch to `printf '%s' "$VAR" | grep -c '^'` or `[ -z "$VAR" ] && N=0 || N=$(echo "$VAR" | wc -l)`. Out of scope for this request.

all_findings.issue: File is stubbed but physically remains on disk; spec Must-Have #8 specified DELETE.
all_findings.recommendation: User should manually `rm ~/.claude/hooks/smart-checkpoint.sh` (bash-safety hook prevents agent deletion). Documented in the stub's header comment.
